items.headline: Google Downtown West moves to on hold
items.summary: Google Downtown West in San Jose has moved from Under Construction to On Hold. The mixed-use project is up to 7.3M sq ft; 4,000+ housing units planned.

items.headline: Council to consider multi-family residential development approval
items.summary: The City Council is scheduled to consider a request to approve construction of a multi-family residential development in Los Gatos. No location, unit count, or other details are provided in the agenda.

items.headline: Study session on zoning and protections for parks and recreation sites
items.summary: City Council will review existing designations, zoning corrections, and state law protections affecting recreational facilities and parkland in Cupertino. The session will consider whether current zoning and protections adequately safeguard these community spaces.

items.headline: Two new single-family homes permitted on Chamberlain Drive
items.summary: Building permits issued for two new 2-story houses at 3550 and 3554 Chamberlain Drive, totaling roughly $1.09 million.

items.headline: Fifth Third Bank permitted to install signage on Lytton Avenue
items.summary: The bank received permits for three illuminated LED wall signs at 250 Lytton Avenue, marking a new branch location in downtown Palo Alto.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers banning chemical pesticides on city property
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission is studying a policy to eliminate chemical pesticide use on all city-owned or leased property. A parallel study on artificial turf policy suggests the city may be rethinking grounds maintenance across parks, facilities, and landscaping.

items.headline: Historic district demolition hearing for 439 Lincoln Avenue building
items.summary: The Historic Resources Board held a public hearing on a demolition application for a building classified as significant within the Professorville Historic District. The recommendation from the board will inform whether the city permits the demolition.

items.headline: Nonprofits present transportation access plan for vulnerable residents
items.summary: The Human Relations Commission heard a presentation from PATMA, a nonprofit organization, about transportation access and mobility support for vulnerable residents in Palo Alto, with a discussion session scheduled for June 11.
